content.claim: Between-school variance in achievement is the third highest among OECD countries, while within-school variance is below the OECD average, indicating that student segregation across schools rather than within schools drives inequality.
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Variance structure is consistent with tracking but does not prove tracking causes the variance; other selection mechanisms (housing, migration, school reputation) could produce similar patterns.
Does not measure whether tracking exacerbates or merely reflects pre-existing inequality in student composition.
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
